Duration: June 2023 – May 2027. Email: divyeshkamalanaban@gmail.com GitHub: https Divyesh-Kamalanaban
LinkedIn: https Portfolio: https Blog: https Phone: +91 8667072079 [SUMMARY] Engineer focused on solving engineering problems with artificial intelligence and embedded systems. Have built AI pipelines for power systems, bioinformatics, and edge devices. Capable of full-stack web development, backend integration, and ML engineering. Enjoys bridging deep learning, machine learning with real-world engineering contexts. Interested in scalable inference, lightweight ML systems, AI/ML pipelines. Pursuing Bachelor of Engineering, Electronics and Communication Engineering at SRM Institute of Science and Technology. Intelligence Engineer by trade, Intelligence Engineer by passion, [PROJECTS]

Developed a continually developing system demonstrating resource-adaptive cryptography on an ESP32-S3 microcontroller. Implemented a dynamic Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) engine that balances high security with real-time stability. Integrated a TinyML decision module using TensorFlow Lite to monitor system load and autonomously switch cryptographic parameters. Deployed ML-DSA-44 and ML-DSA-87 (Dilithium variants) for dynamic security scaling. Resolved critical stack overflow issues and optimized system memory. Developed firmware inculcating custom quantized ML algorithms trained on real-word resource activity data. Ensured system reliability through task-level profiling and adaptive switching. Handled complex C/C++ linker issues within the ESP-IDF framework and coordinated multi-language integration. ://github.com/

Designed a research pipeline for machine-learning-based grid fault detection and localization. Affiliated with Divyesh's internship at NIT Trichy. Generated over 21,000 simulated power grid scenarios including controlled faults and variable loads using pandapower. Trained deep neural networks on 14,000 samples, achieving 95% accuracy in power flow estimation. Implemented ensemble models reaching 92% fault detection and 100% localization accuracy. Enhanced solver performance by integrating a DLPF-based optimization, reducing computation time by 70% compared to Newton–Raphson. ://github.com/ Contributed to Smart India Hackathon 2025 under the bioinformatics category. Built a classification pipeline to identify species from environmental DNA (eDNA) data using machine learning.

Expanded raw dataset from 5 FASTQ species files by applying mutation-based augmentation, yielding 10,000+ synthetic samples. Quantized Nucleotide Transformer models from 32-bit to 4-bit precision, cutting GPU memory usage from 9.5GB to 2.5GB with minimal accuracy loss. Supported multi-stage collaboration including preprocessing, model training, and integration. ://github.com/Divyesh-Kamalanaban/seaquenced [EXPERIENCE] National Institute of Technology (NIT) Trichy — Research Intern Worked on grid-based fault simulation and machine learning model development for smart power systems. Generated a large-scale dataset of grid conditions and explored differential power flow solvers. Contributed to the early design of a data-driven fault detection engine.

Circle Communications — MERN Developer Intern Developed Tortovoice, CRM with invoice modules for a financial and inventory management platform. Worked with MongoDB, Express, React, and Node.js to create end-to-end solutions. Automated repetitive document workflows and integrated API-based file generation systems. ://github.com/Divyesh-Kamalanaban/Tortovoice [SKILLS] [CERTIFICATIONS] GitHub Foundations — : June 2028 Introduction to Software Engineering — : Q1MS2 Programming with JavaScript — : CON85YOUUE3 Python (Basic) — : 843c4c4c5644 Python for Data Science, AI & Development — : 7 AI for Entrepreneurship — Frontend Developer (React) Certificate — : 9de990d8b066 Introduction to Front-End Development — : 81 Problem Solving (Basic) — :
